title: I Trust My Body Completely
subtitle: An affirmation for moving from fighting the body to partnering with it in rest
excerpt: A brief affirmation from Day 29 of a 30-day sleep programme. The statement marks the shift from treating the body as an obstacle to sleep and into treating it as the one that already knows how to rest. The key phrase is 'completely' — not partial, provisional trust, but the full kind.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 29 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the habit of monitoring or blaming the body around sleep is strong — when it feels like the body is the source of the problem rather than a partner in solving it.

content.text: A brief affirmation from Day 29 of a 30-day sleep programme. Day 29 theme: end the battle with sleep. The statement names trust in the body as a present fact rather than a future goal — the body already carries the capacity for rest; it is not broken and does not need to be fixed. The second line names the shift from adversary to partner.
Say it three times, morning or evening. Pause on 'completely' — that is the word that carries the weight.
I trust my body completely. We are learning how to rest together. Sleep is the reunion I have been moving toward.
Adapted from the Day 29 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
